query: I have a sub-workflow called 'Customer: build monthly summary' that groups a customer's orders and emits one summary row. The main workflow pulls orders for 50 customers and calls it with an Execute Workflow node. But instead of 50 summaries I get ONE summary that blends all 50 customers' orders together. The body has a grouping node that's clearly seeing everything at once. How do I make it run per customer?
expected_behavior: Diagnose the cause: the Execute Workflow node is using mode 'all' (the default), so the sub-workflow runs once with all 50 customers' items and the grouping/aggregation node sees everyone at once
Explain mode: all (one run, all N items) vs mode: each (N runs, one item each)

Explain that the split matters precisely because this body assumes it sees exactly one item (per-run aggregation), so mode: each is what matches that assumption — one run per customer, one summary each
Set mode: 'each' on the caller's Execute Workflow node via n8n_update_partial_workflow
Recommend mode: each OVER adding a Loop Over Items node inside the sub-workflow, so the body stays single-item and simple
Optionally note waitForSubWorkflow stays true here since the caller needs the summaries back
Reference SUBWORKFLOW_PATTERNS.md for the all-vs-each detail
notes: Classic all-vs-each trap. Body assumes one item (aggregation), default mode: all feeds it all 50 at once. Fix is mode: each on the caller, and the skill should steer away from an internal Loop Over Items node.
